key_insight: PDAce stabilizes primal-dual optimization for nonlinear convex-concave saddle-point problems by linearizing at convex-combination points and moving extrapolation into the nonlinear mapping space, yielding provable convergence without linesearch.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more robust solver template for large-scale inverse problems and learning systems where nonlinear coupling makes standard primal-dual methods unstable or hard to analyze.
method: Constructs a convex-combination point in the primal update and evaluates the Jacobian of the nonlinear coupling term there.
Transfers extrapolation from variable space to mapping space using the latest convex-combination information.
Uses a Lyapunov-based proof to establish global convergence; the accelerated variant aPDAce improves rates under stronger convexity assumptions.
evidence_signal: From the abstract, the method is theoretically validated with global convergence and an ergodic O(1/N) rate, plus O(1/N^2) and linear convergence in stronger convexity regimes.
watch_item: The abstract reports only theory; practical performance may depend on step-size selection, Jacobian cost, and how well the nonlinear model fits the convex assumptions.
paper_title: A New Primal-Dual Algorithm with Convex Combination and Extrapolation for Convex-Concave Saddle Point Problems with Nonlinear Coupling Term
